dhp256:1: To pass judgment hurriedly doesn’t mean you’re a judge.
dhp256:3: The wise one, weighing both the right judgment & wrong,
dhp256:4:
judges others impartially– unhurriedly, in line with the Dhamma, guarding the Dhamma, guarded by Dhamma, intelligent:
he’s called a judge.
dhp259:3: Whoever –although he’s heard next to nothing–
dhp259:6: he’s one who maintains the Dhamma.
dhp261:1: But one in whom there is truth, restraint,
dhp261:4: he’s called an elder, his impurities disgorged, enlightened.
dhp262:2: or lotus-like coloring does an envious, miserly cheat
dhp263:1: But one in whom this is
dhp263:2: cut through up-rooted wiped out– he’s called exemplary,
dhp264:1: A shaven head doesn’t mean a contemplative.
dhp265:1: But whoever tunes out the dissonance of his evil qualities
dhp266:1: Begging from others doesn’t mean one’s a monk.
dhp266:3: As long as one follows householders’ ways,
dhp266:4: one is no monk at all.
dhp267:1: But whoever puts aside both merit & evil and,
dhp268:2: does someone confused & unknowing turn into a sage.
dhp269:1: But whoever–wise, as if holding the scales, taking the excellent– rejects evil deeds:
dhp269:2: he is a sage, that’s how he’s a sage.
dhp269:3: Whoever can weigh both sides of the world:
dhp269:4: that’s how he’s called a sage.
dhp270:4: for being gentle to all living things.
dhp271:1: on account of your habits & practices, great erudition,
dhp271:2: concentration attainments, secluded dwelling, or the thought,
dhp271:3: ‘I touch the renunciate ease that run-of-the-mill people
dhp271:4:
don’t know’:
ever let yourself get complacent when the ending of effluents is still unattained.
